zh.lifeSummary: 當Dorothy Bindloss於大約1554年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德時，她的父親Sir Robert Bindloss25歲，母親Lady Agnes Harrison21歲。她於1567年與Sir Thomas Braithwaite在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1624年在英國維斯特莫藍德肯達爾享年71歲，埋葬在英國維斯特莫藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dorothy Bindloss于大约1554年出生在英国維斯特莫藍德时，她的父亲Sir Robert Bindloss25岁，母亲Lady Agnes Harrison21岁。她于1567年与Sir Thomas Braithwaite在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1624年在英国維斯特莫藍德肯達爾享年71岁，埋葬在英国維斯特莫藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Bindloss
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: nickname, occupational name perhaps for a wolf hunter, from Middle English binden ‘bind’ + Middle English (Old French) loues ‘wolves’. Plurals are most unusual in phrasal nicknames. The more regular singular form occurs in the name of John Byndeloue, 1327 in Subsidy Rolls (Suffolk), for which see Bensley , and is synonymous with Truslove ( see Trussler ). The preposition in the 1302–3 example could alternatively point to a lost place-name but it is more likely to be a scribal error. The earliest examples are from the NR Yorks and WR Yorks border (Ripon, Sharow, and Langthorpe are almost neighbouring settlements), and the surname persisted in NR Yorks until the late 17th century, but its subsequent history belongs mainly to NW England, where it first appears in the previous century.

variants.name: Bendelow
meanings: English: reduced form of Bendelowes, a variant of Bindloss .

variants.name: Bensley
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): possibly an altered form of Bendelowes with metathesis of -s- and substitution of -ley for -low). Bendelowes is a variant of Bindloss, a nickname perhaps for a wolf hunter, from Middle English binden ‘bind’ + Middle English (Old French) loues ‘wolves’.

variants.name: Subsidy Rolls
meanings: English: (i) see Broker . The MED notes that the -o- could be long. (ii) locative name sometimes perhaps denoting a ‘dweller by the brook’, from an agent derivative of Middle English brok. William le Broker, 1332 in Subsidy Rolls (Sussex), was probably a descendant of Anger atte Broke, 1296 Subsidy Rolls (Sussex). Compare Brook .

variants.name: Binsley
meanings: English: (i) variant of Bindloss with metathesis of -s- and substitution of -ley for -low. Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames records John Binlus alias Binsley, 1655 in Inquisitiones post Mortem (Bedale, NR Yorks), and William Benlowes, Binslow, or Billoes, 1628–49 in Parish Registers (Pickhill, NR Yorks). Tooth (3, p. 37) notes the currency of Bindloes and Bindloss in modern Staffs, where Binnesley also occurs. Compare Bensley . (ii) the Staffs name may alternatively be a variant of Binley with an intrusive -s-.

variants.name: Trussler
meanings: English (Surrey and Sussex; of Norman origin): nickname or occupational name, from Old French trousser ‘to truss, bind, carry off’ + love ‘(she-)wolf’, probably for a wolf-hunter. Compare Manlove .

nameMeanings.name: Dorothy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothee
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

variants.name: Dorothie
meanings: Usual English form of   Dorothea .  The name was not used in the Middle Ages, but was taken up in the 15th century and became common thereafter. It was borne by the American film star  Dorothy Lamour  (  1914–1996 , born  Dorothy Kaumeyer ).

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Dorothy Bindloss was born about 1554, in Westmorland, England, her father, Sir Robert Bindloss, was 25 and her mother, Lady Agnes Harrison, was 21. She married Sir Thomas Braithwaite in 1567, in Westmorland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1624, in Kendal, Westmorland, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Westmorland, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dorothy Bindloss est née environ 1554, à Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Bindloss, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Harrison, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Braithwaite en 1567, à Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1624, à Kendal, Westmorland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Westmorland, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dorothy Bindloss um 1554 in Westmorland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Bindloss 25 und ihre Mutter Lady Agnes Harrison 21. Sie hat 1567 in Westmorland, England, United Kingdom Sir Thomas Braithwaite geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1624 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Kendal, Westmorland, England und wurde in Westmorland, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dorothy Bindloss è nata il circa 1554 nel Westmorland, England, sua padre, Sir Robert Bindloss, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Lady Agnes Harrison, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Braithwaite nel 1567, nel Westmorland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1624, nel Kendal, Westmorland, England, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolta a Westmorland, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Dorothy Bindlossが約1554年にイギリスウエストモーランドで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Bindlossは25歳，母Lady Agnes Harrisonは21歳であった。1567年にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドでSir Thomas Braithwaiteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1624年にイギリスウエストモーランドケンダルで71歳で死去し，イギリスウエストモーランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Dorothy Bindloss(이)가 대략 1554년 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Bindloss(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes Harrison(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1567년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드에서 Sir Thomas Braithwaite(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1624년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 켄다에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 웨스트모를랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dorothy Bindloss nasceu aproximadamente 1554, em Westmorland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Bindloss, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes Harrison, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Braithwaite em 1567, em Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1624, em Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, com 71 anos, e foi sepultada em Westmorland, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dorothy Bindloss родилась около 1554 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Bindloss было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Harrison, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Braithwaite в 1567 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1624 г. в местности кендаль, вестморланд, Англия в возрасте 71 год и была похоронена в местности вестморланд, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dorothy Bindloss nació aproximadamente 1554, en Westmorland, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Bindloss, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Lady Agnes Harrison, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Braithwaite en 1567, en Westmorland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1624, en Kendal, Westmorland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultada en Westmorland, Inglaterra.
